author: AndroidUser_Mumbai
title: Discovery features completely useless
body: Been using Spotify for 2 years on Android. The Discover Weekly playlist shows me the same songs every single week. I've heard all of them before. The algorithm has decided I only listen to Bollywood and refuses to show me anything else even though I actively search for international music. Very disappointing. App quality is fine but discovery is broken.

author: PremiumUser_London
title: Paying for Premium but recommendations are stuck in a loop
body: Paying £9.99 a month and the algorithm keeps recommending me the same artists over and over. When I started Spotify I explored jazz and blues. Now every single recommendation is jazz or blues even though I've since moved to mostly hip hop and R&B. There's clearly no mechanism for the algorithm to update when your taste evolves. Waste of premium money for discovery features.

author: CasualListener_Texas
title: Good app but radio feature needs improvement
body: Love Spotify overall. Interface is clean, sound quality is great. My only real complaint is the radio feature. I've been using it for 18 months and I notice it plays the same 50 songs on rotation no matter what artist I start the radio from. If I start a radio from Bob Dylan I expect folk, rock, singer-songwriter. But after 20 minutes I'm getting the same pop rock songs that show up on every other radio station. No real discovery happening.

author: MusicNerd_Berlin
title: Echo chamber algorithm ruined my music life
body: One star because the algorithm has created such a deep echo chamber that I've actually stopped enjoying music. I used to love discovering new artists. Now my Spotify is just the same 200 songs on rotation. I've tried everything to break out: clearing listening history (found out you can't), creating new playlists, explicitly playing different genres. Nothing breaks the loop. The feedback mechanism only reinforces what you already listen to. Absolutely broken product.

author: FreeUser_Nigeria
title: Free version just pushes popular songs
body: On free tier. The discovery feature just pushes whatever is trending globally. Doesn't matter what I actually listen to - after every session it goes back to Afrobeats hits and global pop. I listen to a lot of alternative and indie music but the algorithm ignores this completely. Feels like the free tier discovery is just a promotion engine for popular music, not actual personalization.

author: GymUser_Sydney
title: Autoplay is predictable to the point of uselessness
body: The gym playlist I made has taken over my entire recommendation profile. I train hard 5 days a week and play that playlist each session. Now autoplay anywhere on Spotify goes straight to high-energy gym music. When I'm cooking or relaxing the last thing I want is 150BPM techno. There should be a way to lock certain playlists out of the recommendation algorithm. Good app otherwise.

author: GenreExplorer_Chicago
title: Tried to explore new genres, algorithm broke completely
body: I spent one week exploring country music, which I'd never really listened to before. After that week, my entire recommendation profile changed to country. My previous recommendations (indie rock, alternative) completely disappeared. I've been trying to get back to my normal recommendations for 3 months. Nothing works. The algorithm is too sensitive to short-term behavior and doesn't have enough memory of long-term preferences.

author: LongTimeUser_2016
title: 8 years as a user, discovery quality has declined significantly
body: I've been using Spotify since 2016. In the early days, Discover Weekly was genuinely incredible - it found music I'd never heard and I loved it. Now in 2024 it's essentially just reshuffling what I already know. I have a theory: as more users joined Spotify, the algorithm became more conservative because it optimized for average satisfaction rather than personalized discovery. The 2024 algorithm serves 600 million users the same safe content. The 2016 algorithm felt like it was making personalized choices just for me.

author: StudentUser_Delhi
title: Good service, bad at actually discovering music
body: Spotify is good for listening to music I already know. But the discovery features don't work well for me. I'm Indian and I listen to a mix of Hindi, Punjabi, and English music. The algorithm doesn't understand how to bridge these and recommend across language boundaries. Discover Weekly is almost entirely English music even though 60% of my listening is Hindi. The cross-language recommendation is nonexistent.

author: PodcastAndMusic
title: Podcasts completely ruined my music recommendations
body: Made the mistake of using Spotify for podcasts. Started listening to true crime podcasts. Now my music Discover Weekly has true crime-adjacent atmospheric and dark music dominating it. I never wanted this connection. The algorithm clearly bleeds podcast content signals into music recommendations. Please separate these completely. I want my music recommendations to be based ONLY on my music listening behavior.

author: NightOwl_Listener
title: Night listening completely dominates recommendations
body: I'm a night person and I do most of my Spotify listening between 10pm-2am. I tend to listen to mellow, atmospheric music at night. This has completely dominated my recommendation profile. During the day when I want energetic music, Spotify keeps pushing mellow night-time music. Time-of-day context should inform recommendations. Night preferences shouldn't override day preferences.

author: AnnoyedPremium_User
title: Autoplay repeat is making me hate songs I used to love
body: Through autoplay and recommendation loops I've now heard 'Blinding Lights' approximately 500 times in the last year. I used to genuinely enjoy that song. Now I can't hear it without wanting to turn Spotify off. The algorithm's obsession with high-performing songs means they get played into the ground. Discovery should mean playing something NEW, not playing popular songs to death. One star because this has actively made my relationship with music worse.

author: MultiGenreUser
title: Algorithm gets confused by eclectic taste
body: I genuinely like a wide range of music. Electronic, jazz, folk, hip-hop, classical. Spotify seems to struggle with eclectic listeners. Instead of embracing my range, it picks one genre that I've listened to recently and doubles down on it for weeks. I'd love a way to tell the algorithm 'I like variety itself, not just one genre'.

author: FamilyAccount_US
title: Kids' music contaminating adult recommendations in family plan
body: I have kids who use my family plan. They listen to a lot of kids' music and animated movie soundtracks on their accounts. Despite having separate accounts, my recommendations started including a lot of family-friendly content and animated movie music. Either accounts are being linked in ways they shouldn't be, or the family plan billing connection creates recommendation overlap. This should absolutely not happen.

author: ClassicalTransition
title: Moving from pop to classical - algorithm refuses to accept it
body: I went through a period where I deliberately transitioned from pop to classical music listening. I wanted to learn to appreciate classical. Spent 3 months almost exclusively listening to classical. My pop recommendations never went away and my classical recommendations never became as sophisticated as they should be for a dedicated listener. The algorithm seemed uncertain whether to believe my 3-month commitment to classical. Taste evolution should be respected.
